title: Chandelier exit rules do not check position direction

evidence.quote: The long exit rule only checks that CurrentPosition is open before comparing close <= exitLevel.
The short exit rule only checks that CurrentPosition is open before comparing close >= exitLevel.
Position exposes IsLong and IsShort helpers that are not used by these side-specific rules.
Tests cover the long rule with a long position, but not with an open short.
Tests cover the short rule with a short position, but not with an open long.

reasoning: The constructors and comments describe side-specific long and short exits, but either rule can fire for any open position. A long Chandelier rule can exit a short position on a favorable price drop, and a short Chandelier rule can exit a long position on a favorable rise if the wrong rule is composed into a strategy.
reproduction: Open a short position, use NewDefaultChandelierExitLongRule on a series where close falls below the long exit level, and IsSatisfied can return true despite the position being short.
recommendation: Require CurrentPosition().IsLong() for the long rule and CurrentPosition().IsShort() for the short rule before checking the exit level.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The tests cover no-position and matching-side positions only; they do not assert that the side-specific rules ignore the opposite position side.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add opposite-side tests: long rule with an open short and short rule with an open long should both return false even when their price thresholds are crossed.
minimumFixScope: pkg/trading/chandelier_exit.go and chandelier exit side-guard tests.
